SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY CROSS COMPLAINANT REESE, RICHARD AN INDIVIDUAL SERVED JUL-18-2012, MAIL AS TO CROSS DEFENDANT RAIZEL SYDELL ENTERPRISES LLC

LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS DEFENDANT RAIZEL SYDELL ENTERPRISES LLC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS DENIED, WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE CLERK PROPERLY ENTERED DEFAULT AND STRUCK RAIZEL SYDELL ENTERPRISES, LLC'S ANSWER TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. "[A] PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO THE ENTRY OF DEFAULT ON THE DATE HE REQUESTS IT IF THERE IS THEN NO RESPONSIVE PLEADING BY THE DEFENDANT ON FILE; AND THAT THE PLAINTIFF CANNOT BE DEPRIVED OF HIS RIGHT TO THE ENTRY OF A DEFAULT MERELY BECAUSE THE CLERK ERRONEOUSLY FAILS TO PERFORM HIS MINISTERIAL DUTY AND DOES NOT ENTER IT WHEN REQUESTED." (GODDARD V. POLLOCK (1974) 37 CAL.APP.3D 137, 142.) PER THE REGISTER OF ACTIONS, ON OCTOBER 5, 2012, MR. REESE SUBMITTED HIS REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT. ON OCTOBER 18, 2012, RAIZEL SYDELL ENTERPRISES FILED ITS ANSWER. (MOTION, LIDDICOAT DECL., 2.) MR. LIDDICOAT'S DECLARATION IS INSUFFICIENT TO SHOW ATTORNEY MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT PER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473(B). (MOTION, LIDDICOAT DECL., 3.) THE COURT SIGNED THE PROPOSED ORDER THAT WAS EMAILED TO THE COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077.
ORDER DENYING CROSS-DEFENDANT RAIZEL SYDELL ENTERPRISES LLC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-26-2012 Continued To Feb-27-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-18-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT RAIZEL SYDELL ENTERPRISES LLC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is Denied, without prejudice. The clerk properly entered default and struck Raizel Sydell Enterprises, LLC's Answer to the Cross-Complaint. "[A] plaintiff is entitled to the entry of default on the date he requests it if there is then no responsive pleading by the defendant on file; and that the plaintiff cannot be deprived of his right to the entry of a default merely because the clerk erroneously fails to perform his ministerial duty and does not enter it when requested." (Goddard v. Pollock (1974) 37 Cal.App.3d 137, 142.) Per the Register of Actions, on October 5, 2012, Mr. Reese submitted his request for entry of default. On October 18, 2012, Raizel Sydell Enterprises filed its Answer. (Motion, Liddicoat Decl., 2.) Mr. Liddicoat's declaration is insufficient to show attorney mistake, inadvertence or excusable neglect per Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b). (Motion, Liddicoat Decl., 3.) The court signed the proposed order that was emailed to the court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077.
